spans.text: eyes that shone in softened flame , With wings and wand , young Fancy
Where shall the heartsick minstrel stray , But ( viewing all things like a dream ) By haunted wood , or wizard stream
Repose herself to hear its soothing plaint
This scene the mistress of the valley held , Fancy , a magic maid ; and at her will , Aerial castles crowned the gleaming hill , Or forests
With fairer tints I can adorn The living landscape , fairer
shades more sweet than real on its breast
The small brook huddling its uneven way
The small brook huddling its uneven way
those they edge the fading west
passing silent through a myrtle grove , Beauty first
does the hurrying pulse unbidden leap
the light bells of the nodding flower
the charm of her delicious dream
the ditty From the lowly cave of Pity
that gathered in the dank abode Fell dripping
to swell , At times was heard a slow and melancholy knell
At times was heard a slow and melancholy knell
A river dark and silent flowed
from lamentation named A river dark and silent flowed
A pale and melancholy man , intent His look upon that drowsy stream he bent , As ever counting
his chilling look Upon a dreadful book , Inscribed with secret characters of woe ; While gibbering imps , as mocking him , appeared , And airy laughter ' mid the dusk was heard
Upon a dreadful book , Inscribed with secret characters of woe
all that valley that so lovely smiled Was changed to a bare champaign , waste and wild
What pale and phantom-horseman rides amain ? "
that magic maid , with aspect bland , It's thine to seize his phantom spear , It's thine his sable trumpet to command , And thrill the inmost heart with shuddering fear
a voice more wildly sweet Than winds that pipe on the forsaken shore
as the prise were won , And now she hailed her favourite son , With energy impatient cried : The weary world is dark and wide , Lo
What is a world of vanities To a world as sweet as ours
to dwell on pleasures flown , When that heart no more shall bound At some kind voice's wellknown sound , My spells thy drooping languor shall relieve , And airy spirits touch thy lonely harp at eve
light his seraph wings , Youth exulting flies before them , Scattering cowslips
high amid the fields of air
And shall the aspiring mind , To Fancy be alone resigned
, her witching lay Too often leads the heart astray
bind Thee , a brother , to mankind
her blameless witchery , Or from the languid bosom tear Each sweet illusion
a softer light Sheds on the rocks and seas of night
our light than day , Fond enthusiast
Chivalry again shall call The champions to her bannered hall !
The bloody leaves of the accursed book
Of the wild and wondrous tale
along the rocky vale , Red fires , mingled with the hail
to hear the gathering tempest roll Beneath her feet
the gathering tempest roll Beneath her feet
the powers of Heaven advance , And wheels
bear God's living chariot through the air
the strain of more than mortal harmonies
sound , over Pity's cave , The willow in the wind shall wave
let them sooth a while thy heart
Vain the magic of his lyre
